_note: Shared-body edges from plan §4 P0 item 4 + addendum A.2. Every symbol below was resolved by scripts/conformance/bounds.py at authoring time; downstream slices MUST re-resolve at run time (round-3 R.4). Absolute .cpp lines are metadata for orientation, never authority.
_ordering: Each edge records the shared body's canonical location, the fan-out set of user types, and whether the write side is symmetric with the read side (a shared body with an asymmetric write is exactly the S2 differ's target).

edges.notes: MPOLYGON inherits HATCH-1 (spline-edge tangent gate asymmetry). Round-3 note: MPOLYGON's own top-level parseDwg is a byte-parallel duplicate (addendum A.2 correction to plan §2.2 'ride free' claim).
HELIX inherits SPLINE-01 scenario-flag bug for free. Top-level HELIX::parseDwg is byte-parallel with SPLINE::parseDwg, needing its own row-walk.

Read-side shared free function; write-side is inlined per caller — so every MTEXT read-side finding may map to 3 sites (MText, Attrib, Attdef) but the write-side must be audited per caller. Round-3 candidate: the R2018 opt-bit 0x02 bug lives in the Attrib/Attdef write path per plan §5 rank 8.
Xline has NO own parseDwg/encodeDwg body (verified via bounds.py NOT FOUND). Inheritance is silent — extract_code.py must guard against reporting Xline as asymmetric.

callers_write: DRW_Table::encodeDwg (also inherits — writes ACAD_TABLE as plain INSERT with oType=7)
edges.notes: The DRW_Table silent-INSERT bug (plan §5 Tier 1 rank 6) lives here. bounds.py: DRW_Table::encodeDwg NOT FOUND — inheritance confirmed.

callers_write: DRW_Solid::encodeDwg (22 lines — DELIBERATELY NOT delegating; plan §5 Tier 1 rank 6 warning)
edges.notes: DRW_Solid::parseDwg is 4 lines (delegation to Trace) but DRW_Solid::encodeDwg is 22 lines (NOT delegating). Asymmetric-shared-body — S2 differ target.

callers_write: DRW_Image::encodeDwg SHADOWS DRW_Line::encodeDwg per drw_entities.h documented warning
edges.notes: Not a shared body per se — an inheritance-shadow. Ledger annotation: audit both symbols independently.
Downstream of §20.4.22 COMMON DIMENSION DATA (round-3 M4: needs an explicit P2 slice). Fan-out fixes 8 types at once.

edges.shared_write_symbol: N/A (no write counterpart; write side hardcodes per emitRecordPreamble stub)
callers_read: 38 sites in drw_objects.cpp (plan estimate: 28 — plan was under; see corrections.readCommonObjectHandles_call_sites for the re-verified derivation)
edges.notes: Every table-record parser MUST route through here per S5.2 discipline. The 5 broken parsers (Layer/Textstyle/Vport/Dimstyle/AppId) don't call it; the 4 clean ones (LType/Block_Record/UCS/View) do.

callers_read: BLOCK CONTROL + 9 UNDOCUMENTED control objects — all 10 through this one body
edges.notes: Addendum A.2 edge (NEW vs plan §4 P0 item 4). Fixes to this body reach all 10 controls at once. Round-3 pre-identified finding: dwgreader.cpp:2690 reads an R2007+ string bit while its own comment says 'ObjControl do not have' it.
Addendum A.2 edge. bounds.py: DRW_DimLinear::parseDwg NOT FOUND — inheritance confirmed. extract_code.py must not report as asymmetry.

edges.shared_symbol: DRW_Image::parseDwg (Wipeout parseDwg is a 3-line total delegation)
edges.notes: Addendum A.2 edge. Asymmetric-shared-body — S2 differ target. This is the exact pattern the plan calls 'shared read + separate write'.
Addendum A.2 edge. DXF-side inheritance closure — P4/parsecode_closure.py MUST subtract this when computing MISSING codes for Attdef, or reports every Attrib case as missing.

corrections.plan_2.2_ride_free_claim: Overstated. MPOLYGON and HELIX inherit parseDwgBoundaryData/parseDwgSplineBody free BUT have byte-parallel top-level parseDwg bodies (drw_entities.cpp:6491, :7307) that need their own row-walks. Fixed here.
corrections.readCommonObjectHandles_call_sites: Plan estimate 28. Re-verified independently 2026-07-18: 43 raw grep hits (--include=*.cpp --include=*.h, .orig excluded) = 1 definition + 4 comment-only mentions + 38 actual call sites. Corrected from this file's prior (incorrect) claim of 40 -- that figure did not reproduce on re-grep.
corrections.seekObjectHandleStream_call_sites: Plan 44 (raw grep hits, uncorrected). Round-3 derivation: 44 = 1 def @drw_objects.cpp:59 + 7 comment-only mentions + 36 actual call sites -- CONFIRMED by independent re-grep 2026-07-18 (exact match: 44 raw, 1 def, 7 comments, 36 calls). Final answer 36 is correct. This file's prior note stated a different, non-reproducing derivation ("38 raw grep hits - 1 def - 1 comment") which did not match a fresh grep (44 raw, not 38) -- corrected here to the verified derivation.
